tips: Did you know? People who budget are 50% more likely to feel in control of their finances.
The 50/30/20 rule: 50% for needs, 30% for wants, 20% for savings. Simple and effective!
Compound interest is called the 'eighth wonder of the world' — it makes your money grow on itself.
An emergency fund of 3-6 months of expenses can save you from debt spirals during tough times.
Paying only the minimum on your credit card can mean you'll be paying for years — and paying way more than you borrowed.
Before any purchase, try the 24-hour rule: wait a day. If you still want it, go for it.
Automating your savings means you save without thinking about it — set it and forget it!
The best time to start investing was yesterday. The second best time is today.
Never share your banking passwords, PINs, or OTPs with anyone — not even your bank will ask for them.
A strong password has at least 12 characters with a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.
Subscriptions you forgot about could be costing you hundreds per year. Do a quick audit!
Simple interest is calculated on the original amount. Compound interest is calculated on the growing total — big difference over time!
Secured loans (like mortgages) usually have lower interest rates than unsecured loans (like credit cards).
Before taking a loan, calculate the total cost — not just the monthly payment.
Index funds let you invest in hundreds of companies at once — great for beginners!
Your credit score affects your ability to borrow — paying bills on time is the #1 way to build it.
Financial literacy is a skill, not a talent. Anyone can learn it with practice!
The latte factor: small daily expenses ($5/day) add up to $1,825/year. Small changes matter.
If an investment sounds too good to be true, it probably is. Always research before committing.
Having a financial goal makes you 42% more likely to achieve it. Write yours down today!
